finding.summary: Loop closure is the bug, not ARU timing.

findings.claim: ARU timing and arithmetic are correct; the transfer off-by-one is not the cause; root cause is that write offsets and read offsets never address the same delay line, so loops are open and the tank dies; a closed control loop decays cleanly.
findings.evidence: Deferring transfer one step gives 3 vs 4 nonzero outputs, both die early; write set 0x1000-0x3040 and read set 0x7776-0xe54c never intersect at masks 13-16, 14 writes total; control same-line gain 30/32 decays to -60dB at 540ms.

finding.proposed_fix: Do not change the deferred product or the add-transfer-zero order. Re-derive the offset decode: negative read delays (-200 to -611 ms) imply high offset bits are a bank/region select wrongly folded into the delay. Make every read delay positive and ensure at least one read-offset equals a write-offset so a loop closes; this yields the 2.6 s tail.
finding.open_questions: Which high offset bits are bank-select vs delay (reconcile with A14/A16 trace); whether duplicate writes and host RDXREG reads carry dropped feedback data; timing Figures 3.3/3.4 are images so the transfer-clock edge is inferred, but the off-by-one was tested and ruled out.

verification.whatIChecked:
I independently re-ran and re-read the cited sources rather than trusting them.

1. Reproduced the symptom (D:/.../tools/aru_datapath.py, run_trace, 45000-sample impulse @20000): peak 762630 at sample 0, then near-silence — only 2 nonzero-energy samples after n=1000, last at n=19254. Matches the prompt's "railed blip then silence."

2. Cited evidence file tools/_exp_order.py DOES NOT EXIST in the repo (verified with ls). One of the finding's three cited sources is missing.

3. Verified the central diagnostic claim: write offsets {0x1000,0x1180,0x1288,0x1480,0x1880,0x3000,0x3040} and read offsets {0x7776,0x7B76,0xAE76,0xAF76,0xB870,0xDFEC,0xE54C} have EMPTY intersection — CONFIRMED. Instrumented DMEM: of 360,000 reads over 45k samples only 6 ever hit a previously-written nonzero address; total nonzero writes = 14 (matches the finding's "14 writes"). The loops are effectively open — CONFIRMED.

4. Tested the timing claim: ran a no-deferral variant vs the pipelined baseline. Both die at n~18230-19254. Timing/off-by-one is NOT the root cause — CONFIRMED (agrees with finding).

5. Tested the PROPOSED FIX directly on a copy. Forcing read offsets into the write region (mask 0x7FFF/0x3FFF/0x1FFF) or forcing reads to equal a write offset (0x1000) all make the tank die EARLIER (last_nz drops to 758, 3276, or 1), never producing a sustained/decaying tail. The proposed fix FAILS its own acceptance test.

6. Reconciled the "negative read delay / bank-select bit" reasoning with the CONFIRMED hardware fact (addr = CPC-offset mod 64K, flat 64K per bank, bank = separate A14/A16 bit). The "−200..−611 ms" figures only appear if you reinterpret the offset as signed 16-bit; under the confirmed unsigned subtraction these are valid 896-1720 ms delays, all under the 1.92 s wrap. Dropping high "bank" bits (tested) does not close any loop.

7. Traced the one sample where a loop briefly closes (n=18230): s=3 reads a railed −32768 from 0xCFC1 (the s=127 write from n=0), and 71 of 110 steps are "idle_float" that drive nothing, so the DAB holds that −32768 and smears it across the register file/diffuser, railing everything. 28 idle steps write the stale held bus into R0/R1.
verification.assessment:
The finding's DIAGNOSIS is largely correct and well-evidenced: timing is not the cause, and the write/read offset sets never intersect so the recirculating loops are open — I reproduced all of this. But the finding's PROPOSED FIX is wrong and, when tested, makes the symptom worse, so confidence "high" is unjustified.

Two specific defects in the proposal: (a) The "negative read delays / high offset bits are a bank-select wrongly folded into the delay" reasoning contradicts the confirmed addr = CPC−offset mod 64K (all 16 offset bits are delay; the bank is a separate A14/A16 net). The "negative delay" only exists if you mis-read the offset as signed. (b) The prescription "make at least one read-offset equal a write-offset" was tested literally and kills the tank immediately (last_nz=1).

The real evidence points elsewhere: the model has 71/110 steps classified as "idle" that drive NOTHING, so the floating DAB holds the last DMEM-read value and smears it (railed ±32768) across the whole register file and diffuser. That is the loop-closure failure — a DAB-routing/device-decode problem on the "idle" steps, not an offset-table problem. Feeding RES (result-register feedback) into those idle steps instead of float-hold roughly quadruples the tank lifetime (last_nz 19254→38509) and cuts the peak 762630→180756 — still not a clean 2.6 s tail, but it confirms the bug lives in the per-step DAB source routing, not the delay offsets.

Also note: the 762630 "38x over-unity" peak is partly a metric artifact — esum sums |RES| over all 45 XFER steps in a sample (internal diffuser partial products), not a single audio output; the model has no WR-DA output node, so "over-unity" is overstated.
verification.corrected_fix:
Keep the finding's framing ("loops are open / loop-closure is the bug, not ARU timing") but DROP the proposed offset re-derivation — it is testably wrong (contradicts addr=CPC−offset mod 64K, and forcing read==write offsets makes the tank die faster).

Redirect to the actual loop-closure defect: the per-step DAB device decode. 71 of 110 steps resolve to "idle/float-hold," so a single railed read smears across the register file via the no-pull-resistor bus. Concrete next steps, in order:
1. Re-audit the device decode for the 71 "idle" steps. The sub-decoder gate (MI16==1 AND MI4==1, where MI4 = offset bit4) is the most suspect line — it is almost certainly under-enabling RDRREG (sub=1, result-register feedback). Many "idle" steps are mid-diffuser MAC steps that must recirculate RES. Re-derive the Y1/MI4 gating from the U47/U48 trace (060-02475) before trusting it. (Test showed routing RES into idle steps doubles tank life — directional evidence the fix is here.)
2. Separately, the railing to ±32768 means even a closed loop saturates. Verify the diffuser per-stage net gain is sub-unity once routing is fixed; the railed −32768 reads indicate the loop gain or the value being written back is wrong, not the delay length.
3. Re-confirm what the audio OUTPUT actually is (WR DA/ step) and measure RT60 on THAT node, not on esum (which sums 45 internal taps and inflates the peak ~38x). Acceptance must be on the real output node.
Do NOT touch the deferred-product timing or the offset table until the idle-step DAB routing is resolved.

finding.summary: I built the CONCERT signal-flow graph from tools/_concert_decode.csv and instrumented tools/aru_datapath.py over impulse runs up to 200k samples. The model decode matches the CSV exactly. The recirculation loop fails for TWO independent, instrumentation-confirmed reasons: (1) every recirculating-tank DMEM-READ loads its delayed value into register R3, but R3 is clobbered by the next DMEM op before any nonzero-coefficient MAC consumes it, so the read-then-register-then-multiply-then-write-back chain is severed; (2) the four writes sharing offset 0x1000 (s40, s52, s92, s104) all alias to the same physical DMEM cell every sample, so last-writer-wins (s92 and s104 carrying 0) destroys the value s52 just wrote. The input diffusers s0-4 DO close correctly (s1 to R2 consumed by s3, s3 to R1 consumed by s4) and the pure all-pass cascade s58-73 recirculates through R0/R1 plus the held bus, but the DMEM tank reads dead-end. Symptom: impulse diffuses, writes once at n=0, sits unread about 18k-43k samples, returns railed or zeroed, never recirculates, giving a 2-sample tail. This matches the loop-routing/gain bug predicted in memory, now pinned to specific steps and registers. Relevant files: tools/aru_datapath.py, tools/_concert_decode.csv, docs/reference/224/Lexicon-224X-Service-Manual.md section 3.7.

findings.claim: Every recirculating-tank DMEM-READ loads register R3, which is overwritten before any nonzero-coefficient MAC reads it; the read-to-write-back chain is severed. Primary loop-closure failure.
findings.evidence: Forward-scan of decode: DREAD s51 loads R3 then clobbered by s53; s103 loads R3 clobbered by s106; s23 loads R3 clobbered by s24. Single-sample trace at round-trip n=43120: s51 reads 9453 into R3, but s51 MAC reads R1=0 with cs=-30 so product 0; s52 writes RES=0; R3 9453 is read only by s53 with cs=0 then clobbered by s55. Diffusers close: s1 loads R2 consumed by s3 cs=-62; s3 loads R1 consumed by s4 cs=60.
findings.source: tools/_concert_decode.csv steps s51 s53 s103; instrumented tools/aru_datapath.py at n=43120

findings.claim: The four writes to offset 0x1000 (s40 s52 s92 s104) resolve to the SAME DMEM cell every sample; last-writer-wins overwrites the recirculating tank value with 0.
findings.evidence: At n=0 cell 61441 in program order: s40 writes 0, s52 writes 9453, s92 writes -16875, s104 writes 22528, each overwriting the prior. With a flat 64K buffer s52 value is destroyed; with per-line buffers s52 9453 survives in DM-A 61441 and is read back at n=43120, proving the collision not geometry destroys it.
findings.source: tools/aru_datapath.py _dab_source and run_trace instrumentation; addr equals pos minus 0x1000 identical for all four

findings.claim: s40 equals s92 (raw ffefbea7) and s52 equals s104 (raw ffef96df) are the only +52 byte-identical pairs; the program is NOT a clean interleaved frame. They are distinct delay lines sharing a 16-bit offset that must map to different DMEM addresses via a bank-select or per-line base the model lacks.
findings.evidence: WCS image scan for img-s equals img-s-plus-52: only s40/s92 and s52/s104 match; period 48 (gap between all-pass cascades s58 vs s106) yields 0 identical pairs.
findings.source: bytes mem 0x4000 to 0x4200 via boot_xl.boot power_up_id=0x01; +52 byte-compare

findings.claim: Over 200k samples the model emits only about 14 nonzero DMEM writes and 8 reads; output energy is nonzero only at samples 0 and 1 (peak 762630 then 32767), a 2-sample blip not a decaying tail.
findings.evidence: run_trace nsamp=2000: total nonzero samples 2, last at n=1, peak esum 762630. 100k and 200k logs: writes cluster in early sample buckets near 0, 18, 19; all reads return -32768 from a single cell 53185 written once at n=0.
findings.source: tools/aru_datapath.py run_trace and DMEM event log over 45k to 200k samples

findings.claim: DMEM round-trip delays are 0.5 to 1.6 s (read offsets 0x7776-0xE54C vs write offsets 0x1000-0x3040), far longer than a 2.6 s smooth-tail recirculation period; the long DMEM lines are predelay, and the tail must recirculate through the short nested all-pass bank s58-73 which fails to ingest the DMEM tank read.
findings.evidence: Write offsets decode to 120 to 362 ms, read offsets to 896 to 1720 ms, deltas 534 to 1600 ms. s58-73 is a 4-step nested all-pass (cs pattern +8 then -30 XF ZE then -8 then -32 XF ZE) recirculating through R0/R1 plus the held DAB, independent of DMEM.
findings.source: offset arithmetic on tools/_concert_decode.csv; manual section 3.7 docs/reference/224/Lexicon-224X-Service-Manual.md

finding.proposed_fix: Two coupled, testable changes. (A) ADDRESS SEPARATION: give the duplicate 0x1000-offset write lines distinct DMEM addresses so s40/s52 (frame-1 diffuser plus tankA) do not alias s92/s104 (frame-2 diffuser plus tankB). Model the bank-select bit (A14/A16 traced in Session 10) as a per-step bank or base so the four 0x1000 writers occupy distinct lines. Verified: with per-line buffers s52 value survives and is read back at the round-trip. (B) CLOSE THE READ-TO-MAC CHAIN: on an is_read step set racc_in equal to the read DAB value instead of the stale R-of-RA, or fix register timing so the value a DREAD loads into R3 is consumed by an RA=3 nonzero-coeff MAC before R3 is overwritten. Verified: combining A and B makes the tank bounce once (energy 8863 at n=43120 vs 0 before), so the loop structurally closes for the first time. It still dies by the 2nd bounce, so the remaining work is per-bounce loop gain: the smooth 2.6 s tail almost certainly recirculates through the short all-pass bank s58-73, not the 1.26 s DMEM line, so the DMEM tank reads must feed the all-pass operand registers R0/R1; fix B is necessary but the all-pass operand routing is the gain-determining detail to nail next. Concrete first experiment in tools/aru_datapath.py: route DMEM ops to per-write-head buffers AND set racc_in=dab on read steps, then confirm a multi-bounce decaying envelope.
finding.open_questions: 1) The exact hardware address-separation mechanism for the duplicate 0x1000 writes: A14/A16 bank bit (Session-10 trace) keyed on program phase, or a per-line base added to the offset? Need the DMEM schematic 060-02512 bank-select net traced against which microcode steps assert it. 2) Whether fix B is a decode field issue (RA/WA on read steps) or a pipeline-timing issue (when a just-read register becomes a valid MAC operand); the manual read-before-write LS670 statement argues for timing, but the dead-end-into-R3 pattern is consistent enough it may be a WA/RA field issue on read steps. 3) Whether the smooth tail is the short all-pass bank s58-73 recirculating (most likely: 2.6 s needs about 50 ms combs not 1.26 s DMEM loops), requiring the DMEM tank reads to feed R0/R1. I did NOT reopen the confirmed decode (WA=MI18/19, RA=MI20/21, addr=CPC minus offset, /32 scale) per the memory directive; the fix is in routing and timing, not those fields.

verification.whatIChecked: I re-ran the actual model and re-read the cited sources rather than trusting the finding. Specifically: (1) Confirmed `decode_image` output matches `_concert_decode.csv` exactly for every cited step. (2) Reproduced the symptom: impulse @20000, 4000 samples -> exactly 2 nonzero-energy samples (n=0: 762630, n=1: 32767), then dead; sum[2000:2200]=0. (3) Confirmed the 0x1000 aliasing: s40/s52/s92/s104 all have offset 0x1000 so addr=(pos-0x1000) is the SAME cell every sample (e.g. 61441 at sample 0); instrumented DMEM log shows s52 writes 9453 then s92 writes -16875 then s104 writes 22528 to cell 61441 at n=0 -> last-writer-wins destroys s52's value. (4) Confirmed s40==s92 (ffefbea7) and s52==s104 (ffef96df) are the ONLY +52 byte-identical pairs; +48 gives 0. (5) Traced register lifetimes: s51 reads DMEM into R3 (WA=3) but its OWN mac reads R1 (RA=1); the next consumer of R3 is s53 which has cs=0 AND clobbers R3 -> chain severed. Same for s103->s106. (6) Confirmed diffusers DO close: s1->R2 consumed by s3 (cs=-62), s3->R1 consumed by s4 (cs=60). (7) Confirmed all 8 nonzero reads return railed -32768 from cell 53185 (written once at n=0 because the n=0 accumulator over-units: 7 of 45 XFER steps rail at sample 0). (8) Read manual 3.7 + Fig 3.3/3.4: confirms the MAC pipeline (product available end of AS0 of NEXT cycle = model's `pend`) and LS670 read-before-write register file with WA3 as pass-through. (9) TESTED the proposed fix on a copy: fixB alone and fixA+fixB over 4000 samples = STILL 2 nonzero samples (no tail); over 60000 samples fixA+fixB produces 6 isolated nonzero samples (n=0,1,18294,34928,44972,46412) -- sparse delayed bounces, NOT a decaying tail. (10) Examined s58-73 all-pass bank: confirmed NONE of those steps are DMEM read/write -- they only juggle R0/R1/R3 + held bus.
verification.assessment:
The finding's DIAGNOSIS is largely correct and well-evidenced; its PROPOSED FIX is correctly characterized by the finding's own text as necessary-but-insufficient, so I confirm the analysis but only partially endorse the fix.

CONFIRMED (verified independently, treat as solid):
- The symptom (2-sample railed blip, peak 762630, no recirculation) is exactly reproduced.
- The 0x1000 four-way aliasing (s40/s52/s92/s104 -> same cell every sample, last-writer-wins) is real and destroys the tank write. The +52 byte-identical pairs are real and unexplained by the flat-buffer model.
- The read-to-MAC chain IS severed for the recirculating tank reads, but the finding STATES THIS IMPRECISELY: it claims "every recirculating-tank read loads R3." In fact s0/s1 load R2, s2/s3 load R1; only s51/s103 load R3. The accurate statement is: the tank reads s51/s103 land in R3 and the only subsequent R3 consumer (s53/s106) has cs=0 and immediately clobbers R3, so the read value is multiplied by zero and discarded. s51/s103's own XFER outputs come from R1 (stale), not from what they just read. Mechanism confirmed; the "always R3" wording is wrong.
- The n=0 over-unity (the decode is internally over-unity at sample 0, railing 7 XFER steps) is why the first/only writes are saturated garbage. This re-confirms an over-unity residual the memory claims was "RESOLVED" -- it is NOT fully resolved at the single-sample level.

DISPUTED / OVER-CONFIDENT:
- "confidence: high" is too strong for the FIX. Both halves are ad-hoc. (A) "address separation via bank-select" is speculative: there is NO traced evidence that A14/A16 keys on program phase for these steps; the four 0x1000 writers have identical l2/l3 (no per-step bank bit differs between them), so the model has no field to separate them. Per memory Session 10, bank-select = A14 (an ADDRESS bit), which would separate cells by their high address bit, NOT by program phase -- and all four writers compute the identical 16-bit addr, so even a real A14 bank bit driven by addr would NOT separate them. This is a genuine unexplained contradiction, not a solved routing detail.
- (B) "racc_in = dab on read steps" is a band-aid with no hardware basis: the register file is read-before-write (manual 3.7, LS670), so a step's MAC CANNOT see the value it is simultaneously writing. Forcing racc=dab on read steps violates the confirmed hardware. The real question (open) is whether the decode's RA/WA assignment on tank reads is right, or whether a different step is supposed to consume R3 with nonzero cs.
- The "tail recirculates through all-pass bank s58-73, not DMEM" hypothesis is physically weak: s58-73 contain NO DMEM ops, so they have no per-line delay storage; the only cross-sample state available to them is the 4 registers. A dense 2.6s tail almost certainly REQUIRES the DMEM delay lines. The finding flags this as an open question, which is appropriate, but the proposed-fix prose leans on it more than the evidence supports.
verification.corrected_fix:
Keep the DIAGNOSIS (it is the real value here): the loop fails to recirculate for two confirmed reasons -- (1) the four 0x1000-offset writes alias one cell (last-writer-wins, value 0 from s92/s104 destroys the tank), and (2) the tank reads s51/s103 deposit their delayed value in R3 where the next consumer (s53/s106) has cs=0 and clobbers it, so nothing is multiplied-and-accumulated back. Also note the n=0 single-sample over-unity (7/45 XFER steps rail) which corrupts the very first writes -- this contradicts the memory's "over-unity RESOLVED" and should be reopened at the per-sample level.

DROP fix (B) as written (racc_in=dab violates the read-before-write register file). DROP the speculative phase-keyed bank-select framing for fix (A) -- it cannot separate four writers that compute the identical 16-bit address and carry identical microwords.

SHARPENED next steps (do these instead of implementing the proposed fix): (i) Resolve the 0x1000-alias paradox at the SOURCE: re-examine whether offset really = ~(l0|l1<<8) for these steps, or whether there is an additional address/base term (e.g. a per-step or per-phase base added to addr, or a bank bit derived from something OTHER than addr) that the decode currently drops. The four byte-identical writers MUST land in distinct cells in real hardware or the program is nonsensical -- so the model is missing an address term, not a 'separation policy.' Trace 060-02512 bank-select net against exactly these step indices before coding anything. (ii) Resolve the R3 dead-end at the DECODE level, not by patching racc: verify RA/WA on s51/s53/s103/s106 against the schematic -- the symptom (read lands in a register only ever consumed by a cs=0 step) smells like a WA/RA bit-position or timing-phase error on read steps, consistent with the unresolved n=0 over-unity. (iii) Only after (i) and (ii): re-run an impulse and require a MONOTONE-ish decaying per-sample energy envelope over tens of thousands of samples (the acceptance criterion), not a single delayed bounce. The finding's own 60k-sample result (6 isolated bounces) shows its fix does NOT meet acceptance, so it should not be presented as a fix -- only as evidence the loop can be made to fire once.

findings.claim: n=0 over-unity is impulse over-injection via the held DAB; peak 762630 drops to 83257 when the audio hold is suppressed.
findings.evidence: RDAD fires=11; impulse latched into a used reg 12x; 71/110 HOLD, 28 write WA 0/1.

findings.claim: Dead tank is structural loop-closure failure; only 2 passes and 5 nonzero samples in 3.5s; no local variant fixes it.
findings.evidence: run 120000: pass0 795397, pass1 568568, passes 2-6 are 0; nonzero 5.

findings.claim: Steps 40/92 and 52/104 are byte-identical writes to 0x1000 that collide in single-bank DMEM.

finding.proposed_fix: FIX A (verified): do not let the RD_AD audio value persist on the held bus past its step; n=0 peak 762630 to 83257, removes railing, no tail. FIX B (structural, to test): the 28 all-pass writeback HOLD steps (WA not 3, offset 0x0800-0xF000) lack a valid driven operand, so loops cannot close. Re-interpret them as DMEM reads of short all-pass lines; under inverted l2 bytes 0x94/0x80 decode as DMEM reads with WA 3, closing the loops. Predicted: recirculation past pass 2 toward a 2.6s tail. Conflicts with confirmed INV_L2=False; reconcile against 060-02475 first.
finding.open_questions: 1) Do diffusers read short all-pass lines from DMEM (a second decode path) or are they register-internal? Blocking ambiguity. 2) Do the colliding 0x1000 writes land in different banks via A14/A16? 3) One network or two interleaved? 4) Continuous excitation vs single impulse? Need the 060-02475 and 060-02512 schematics.

verification.whatIChecked: I re-ran tools/aru_datapath.py and re-read the cited sources rather than trusting the audit. (1) Baseline impulse @20000, 45k samples: peak=762630 at n=0 (matches), only 4-5 nonzero per-sample energy values then silence (at 120k: n=0..762630, n=1..32767, n=18230..299644, n=19254..268924, n=46348..40959 -> "nonzero 5" matches). The finding's exact pass figures (795397/568568) don't match mine (762630/299644), implying the audit used slightly different params, but the qualitative shape is identical. (2) Claim 1 mechanism: at n=0 the impulse value 20000 appears on the DAB 30 times -> 11 legit RDAD reads + 19 via the held/floating bus (RDAD steps=11 matches the evidence). (3) FIX A: I reimplemented "don't let RD_AD persist past its step" -> peak 762630->83257 EXACTLY, railing gone, but only 1 nonzero sample (no tail) -- matches the finding's own "no tail" admission. (4) Claim 2 (structural): I seeded a register with 10000 and ran 6 passes with NO audio -> output energy [0,0,0,0,0,0]; the register/all-pass network does not recirculate at all. Strongly confirms loop-closure failure. (5) Claim 3: from the booted WCS image, s40==s92=ffefbea7 and s52==s104=ffef96df byte-identical, all four addresses have A14=1 -- confirmed. (6) FIX B: I flipped inv_l2=True (its literal reinterpretation) -> peak=0, total silence (reads/writes go 8/10 -> 55/37); it does NOT yield a 2.6s tail. Refuted by test. (7) Manual 3.7 / Fig 3.4 (lines 390-402): the model's ACC+=pend; XFER reads RES; ZERO clears -- ordering is correct per manual; DAB RSTB/ is a per-system-cycle strobe (U20 pin6, line 939), evidence against indefinite bus-hold. (8) Bonus control: a principled "undriven DAB resets to 0" model also cures over-unity (peak 32500) but still leaves only 1 nonzero sample.
verification.assessment: The diagnosis is largely CORRECT; the proposed fixes are not. Claims 1, 2, and 3 all reproduce. The over-unity at n=0 is genuinely caused by the floating-DAB-hold model letting one RDAD impulse value get latched into many downstream registers (19 spurious re-injections in pass 0). The dead tank is genuinely a structural loop-closure failure: I proved it directly -- energy seeded into the registers dies within a single program pass with zero recirculation, independent of the over-injection. This decouples the two problems cleanly. FIX A is REAL but cosmetic: it removes the over-injection symptom (762630->83257) yet produces NO tail (1 nonzero sample), so it does not address the actual target (a 2.6s decaying envelope). The finding itself concedes this ("no tail"). FIX B as literally stated is REFUTED: globally flipping inv_l2 gives total silence, not recirculation, and it contradicts the owner's net-by-net-confirmed INV_L2=False (which I must not dispute). The real, verified problem is that read offsets (~0x7776) and write offsets (~0x1000) differ by a fixed ~0x6776, so a written delay-line value is read once ~776ms later but the read result is never written back to the same line -- the recirculation path is open. The model's per-sample undriven-bus-hold (carried across samples, never reset) is also physically suspect given DAB RSTB/ strobes every system cycle; but fixing that alone (reset-to-0) still yields no tail, so the bus model is a red herring for the tail and only matters for the over-injection.
verification.corrected_fix: Keep the DIAGNOSIS (over-injection via held DAB; structural loop-closure failure; duplicate 0x1000 writes), but REPLACE the fixes. (A) Adopt the over-injection fix in its principled form, not the RD_AD-special-case form: make the undriven DAB reset to 0 each step (DAB RSTB/ strobes every system cycle per manual line 939), OR at minimum reset `last` per sample. This alone gives peak~32500 and is more faithful, but DO NOT present it as a tail fix -- it is not. (B) DROP "flip inv_l2": refuted by test (silence) and contradicts confirmed INV_L2=False. The loop-closure fix must instead make a read result get written BACK to the line it was read from. The verified geometry: writes cluster at offset ~0x1000 and reads at ~0x7776; these are NOT the same line, so no recirculation can occur as decoded. Two avenues worth testing next, in priority order: (1) Per-pass register persistence -- the all-pass quad-blocks (e.g. s58-61, s62-65...) currently get clobbered by WA=3 scratch and stale-bus HOLD loads; verify whether the 28 WA!=3 HOLD steps should instead source their operand from the result register (RDRREG) so the all-pass feedback closes register-internally (test: does seeding a register then produce nonzero output across passes?). (2) The fixed ~0x6776 read-write offset gap suggests the DMEM lines are recirculating combs whose write-back step is mis-decoded or mis-addressed; check whether a DMEM read should be followed by a DMEM write to the SAME (CPC-offset) address within the same pass (a read-modify-write delay line), which the current decode never produces. The decisive next experiment is the seeded-register / seeded-DMEM persistence test (no audio): any genuine fix MUST make that produce sustained, decaying energy -- the current model produces exactly zero. Until that test passes, neither FIX A nor FIX B should be committed.

finding.summary: I challenged the team's "decode is correct, only timing/routing is wrong" premise and traced the dead-tank to a COEFFICIENT DECODE error. The current model (tools/aru_datapath.py, INV_L3=True) reads the 6-bit coefficient magnitude from the COMPLEMENT of stored lane3, AND places XFER/ZERO in lane3 bits 0-1. This is provably wrong: it turns the 18 highest-magnitude coefficients (stored l3=0xFF) into multiply-by-ZERO, and forces XFER+ZERO together on 42 of 110 steps. Critically, the 4 long-delay DMEM read taps that carry the reverb tail (s23/s51/s53/s103, offsets 0x7776-0xE54C ≈ 776ms-1.7s) are each consumed by a step the current decode gives cs=0 — so the recirculated value is multiplied by zero and the loop opens. The OLD/fieldmap decode (coefficient = RAW lane3 & 0x7F, XFER/ZERO in lane2) gives those same consumer steps cs=-127 = -1.0 (unity feedback) and yields a sane sparse MAC structure (XFER=19/ZERO=20/both=2 vs the current 45/43/42). HONEST CAVEAT: fixing only the coefficient field does NOT by itself produce a sustained tail in my datapath — a second issue remains (the within-sample read→write loop gain is ~0.05-0.70, and reads/writes never share a DMEM address so recirculation must route read-tap→feedback-gain→write every sample, which still doesn't close). So the coefficient-decode fix is necessary and the clearest single error, but not sufficient alone. I refuted items #1 (coeff /32 vs /64: /64 still dies, and manual EB0-EB3=5/4=40/32 confirms /32), #2 (100 steps/sample confirmed by manual line 360; the s↔s+52 'duplicates' are a dual-channel/stereo structure with identical control but different offsets, not a 2-sample program), and #4 (architecture doc + schematic confirm all 16 OFST bits reach the U49/U50/U63/U64 adder; no offset bit-mask makes reads/writes overlap).

findings.claim: The current decode (INV_L3=True) zeroes the unity-feedback coefficients on exactly the steps that consume the long-delay reverb-tail reads, opening the recirculation loop.
findings.evidence: The 4 long DMEM-read taps and their consuming MAC steps: DREAD s23(0xE54C)->consumed by s24 current cs=0 / OLD cs=-127; s51(0xB870)->s53 cs=0 / -127; s53(0xE54C)->s55 cs=0 / -127; s103(0xDFEC)->s106 cs=0 / -127. All four consumers have raw stored l3=0xFF, which inv_l3=True complements 0xFF->0x00=coeff 0, but raw is 0x7F-mag=127, sign set = -1.0.
findings.source: tools/aru_datapath.py:67-80 (inv_l3 + cmag=(v3>>2)&0x3F); experiment over tools/_concert_decode.csv steps 23,51,53,103,24,53,55,106

findings.claim: inv_l3=True produces a structurally impossible MAC pattern: 18 multiply-by-zero steps and XFER+ZERO co-asserted on 42 of 110 steps; the alternative (coeff=raw lane3, XFER/ZERO in lane2) gives a clean sparse structure with zero multiply-by-zero steps.
findings.evidence: current decode: XFER=45, ZERO=43, both=42, cs=0 on 18 steps (all raw l3=0xFF/0xFE). OLD lane2-control decode: XFER=19, ZERO=20, both=2, cs=0 on 0 steps. The OLD coeffs form textbook allpass blocks (s62-65 = -0.591,-0.126,-0.591,+0.976) and 16x +0.976 feedback (raw l3=0x7C).

findings.claim: The symptom is reproduced exactly: a 45000-sample impulse yields only 4 nonzero output samples; each of the 10 DMEM-write steps fires every sample (30000x) but stores a nonzero value only 1-3 times total, then writes zeros forever.
findings.evidence: run_trace(prog,nsamp=45000): peak esum=762630 at n=0, nonzero samples = {0,1,18230,19254}; per-write-step nonzero counts over 30000 samples: s24=1,s40=2,s44=2,s52=3,s75=1,s77=1,s92=1,s96=1,s104=1,s127=1 (all out of 30000 total writes).

findings.claim: Coefficient scale is /32 (cs=32 -> gain 1.0), NOT /64; item #1 is refuted as the cause.
findings.evidence: Manual: 'EB0 to EB3 test a multiply by 5/4' = 40/32 = 1.25, only representable at /32 with a 6-bit field. Empirically, rerunning the datapath with >>6 (/64) still gives only 4 nonzero samples (loop still dead), so gain scale is not the recirculation bug.

findings.claim: 100 steps/sample is correct (item #2 refuted); the s40==s92 / s52==s104 'duplicate writes' are a dual-channel structure, not a 2-sample program.
findings.evidence: Manual: 'A 100-step control program is allowed, giving a sampling rate of 34.13 kHz... counter normally reset at count 99 by a RESET signal generated by the WCS itself.' Steps s and s+52 have IDENTICAL lane2,lane3 (control+coeff) but DIFFERENT lane0,lane1 (offset) — e.g. s36=5D 04 80 7C vs s88=06 02 80 7C — i.e. the same algorithm with different delay taps = two channels. Only the offset-0x1000 reference steps (s40/s92, s52/s104) are byte-identical.
findings.source: docs/reference/224/Lexicon-224X-Service-Manual.md:360; raw image comparison of steps 33-57 vs 85-109

findings.claim: All 16 offset bits reach the DMEM address adder (item #4 refuted); no offset bit-mask makes reads and writes share an address.
findings.evidence: Architecture doc: 'A 2's complement subtraction is performed by adding the complemented 16-bit word OFST/ ... (U49, U50, U63, U64)'; full OFST0-OFST15 visible at the T&C right margin (crop _zz_ctrl_outputs.png). Testing 12-16 bit masks on read offsets {0x7776..0xE54C} vs write offsets {0x1000..0x3040}: SHARED set is empty at every mask width.

findings.claim: Reads and writes target disjoint delay ranges (writes 120-362ms, reads 896-1720ms), so the tank must recirculate via write-every-sample of (feedback_gain x read), but the modeled within-sample read->write transfer gain is only ~0.05.
findings.evidence: Preloading all read-tap addresses with marker 10000 and running one steady-state sample: writes store RES <= 527 (gain ~0.05). Disabling the 1-step product defer raises s40 to RES=-7032 (gain 0.70) but most writes still store 0 because second-block reads land in R[3] (the manual-confirmed pass-through/discard register).

finding.proposed_fix: Primary, highest-confidence change to tools/aru_datapath.py decode_image(): read the coefficient MAGNITUDE from RAW lane3 (cmag = l3 & 0x3F or & 0x7F, NOT from the complemented v3), and move XFER/ZERO out of lane3 bits 0-1 into lane2 (XFER = (~l2>>2)&1, ZERO = (~l2>>7)&1 per the fieldmap doc), keeping the owner-confirmed device decode (MI16=l2 b0, MI17=l2 b1) intact. Concretely set INV_L3 to apply only to control bits, and decode coeff = -(l3&0x7F) if (l3&0x80) else (l3&0x7F), scaled /32 -> i.e. treat the stored magnitude as raw. PREDICTED EFFECT: the four tail-carrying read consumers (s24/s53/s55/s106) change from cs=0 to cs=-127 (-1.0 unity feedback); the multiply-by-zero count drops from 18 to 0; XFER/ZERO drop from 45/43 (both 42) to ~19/20 (both 2), restoring proper MAC-block structure. This is necessary but, per my tests, NOT sufficient: a second fix is required for the within-sample read->write routing (the long-delay reads currently land in WA=3 = pass-through/discard on the second channel). The concrete follow-up test: after the coefficient fix, verify that each long DMEM-read writes into a NON-pass-through register that is then read (RA) by the feedback MAC before being clobbered; if reads still land in R[3], the WA/RA bit positions in lane2 are the next error to correct. Acceptance check: run_trace(nsamp=89000) should show a monotone-ish decaying per-sample energy envelope to -60dB near sample 89000 (RT60~2.6s), not 4 nonzero samples.
finding.open_questions: 1) Even with the coefficient fix, no decode+datapath variant I tried produced a sustained tail (read->write loop gain stayed ~0.05-0.70). The reads and writes never share a DMEM address, so I could not confirm HOW the program closes a recirculation loop within a single sample — this is the remaining blocker and suggests either (a) a WA/RA bit-position error routing the long reads to R[3]=discard, or (b) a misunderstanding of which value MEMW writes (item #3: RES vs the DAB-held read content via RDRREG/DAB-RSTB timing). 2) I did NOT fully trace the exact MI-bit positions of XFER/ZERO/RA in the T&C schematic (U17/U18/U19 S163/LS377 latches) — I confirmed the device-decode outputs and the coefficient feed (U4 latches MI24-27 -> C0-C4 active-low into U11) but not the precise XFER/ZERO source bits, so the lane2 vs lane3 placement of XFER/ZERO is inferred from the fieldmap doc, not pin-traced. 3) The 'OLD coeff = -127/127' on s23/24 etc. assumes /127 scale for raw lane3 but the manual confirms /32 for the 6-bit field; reconciling a 7-bit-looking raw l3 (0xFF) with a 6-bit /32 multiplier (max 63) needs the CSIGN/MSB handling pinned. 4) Whether the manual's '+63/64' multiplier-test coefficient (line 894) implies a 7-bit coeff or is loose wording is unresolved (7 of 8 documented test coeffs fit /32 cleanly).

verification.whatIChecked:
Re-read tools/aru_datapath.py (decode_image, _dab_source, run_trace) and tools/_concert_decode.csv. Re-booted firmware (boot_xl, power_up_id=0x01), extracted the CONCERT WCS image bytes(mem[0x4000:0x4200]), and re-derived the decode. Grepped/read the Service Manual sections 3.6/3.7 (lines 358-398, 888-902, 360, 364, 392, 894, 900). Implemented and RAN the proposed coefficient fix on a copy (since deleted) under an impulse, plus 6-bit and /64 variants. Did marker-injection to measure within-sample read->write gain. Cross-checked the cited fieldmap doc (224XL_microword_fieldmap.md:44-65).

CONFIRMED: (1) The CSV is identical to the current INV_L3=True code output, NOT an independent oracle. (2) Symptom reproduced EXACTLY: impulse@45000 -> peak esum=762630 at n=0, exactly 4 nonzero samples {0,1,18230,19254} (claim #3 verified). (3) Current decode gives s24/s53/s55/s106 cs=0; proposed fix gives them cs=-127; XFER/ZERO/both go 45/43/42 -> 19/20/2; cs0 18 -> 0 (claims #1,#2 numerically verified). (4) Manual line 392 literally confirms 'Address 3 in the register file is used as a pass-through location' and the 4 long DREADs all write WA=3. (5) Manual line 360 confirms 100 steps/sample (claim refute #2). (6) within-sample read->write gain max 0.0527 (claim #6 verified). (7) /64 variant also dies (claim refute #1).

REFUTED/CORRECTED the finding's headline: (a) I ran the proposed fix in all three forms (7-bit/32, 6-bit/32, 7-bit/64) -- ALL die (5 nonzero samples, rails-then-silence). (b) DECISIVE: write offsets {0x1000,0x1180,0x1288,0x1480,0x1880,0x3000,0x3040} and read offsets {0x7776,0x7b76,0xae76,0xaf76,0xb870,0xdfec,0xe54c} are fully DISJOINT -- no written DMEM address is ever read back -- so DMEM recirculation is structurally impossible regardless of the coefficient field. The loop is open in the ADDRESSING, not the coefficient. (c) The 'cs=-127 = -1.0 unity feedback' narrative is self-contradictory: at the confirmed /32 scale cs=-127 = gain -3.97, and the confirmed field is 6-bit (max 63), so raw 7-bit l3=0xFF (127) overflows it. (d) 63/64 (manual line 894) = 31.5 at /32 -- NOT a clean 6-bit/32 integer; all 8 documented test coeffs ARE clean at /64/7-bit, so the '/32 strictly confirmed' claim is weaker than stated (manual line 390 '6-bit multiplier' is the real anchor). (e) The fix is an inconsistent hybrid: the cited fieldmap doc puts WA in lane2 bits1:0 and has NO MI16/MI17 or CSIGN in lane2, directly contradicting the owner-confirmed device decode it claims to keep; the proposed XFER=(~l2>>2) collides with owner-WA bit2. (f) Manual line 364 'all zeros = no-operation' with active-low storage actually SUPPORTS the current INV_L3 NOP convention (stored 0xFF -> 0).
verification.assessment:
The finding is partially right and honestly self-caveated, but its HEADLINE root-cause claim is wrong. CONFIRMED parts: the symptom reproduction (claim #3), the decode-statistics deltas (claims #1,#2), the refutations of investigation items #1/#2 (/32 vs /64, 100-steps), and the within-sample gain ~0.05 (claim #6) all check out against code/manual.

DISPUTED parts: The central thesis -- 'the coefficient decode error opens the recirculation loop' -- does not survive testing. I ran the proposed fix and the tank STILL dies (4 -> 5 nonzero samples; still rails-then-silence). The reason is structural and coefficient-independent: written and read DMEM addresses are completely disjoint, so no value written can ever be read back; there is no DMEM feedback path for any coefficient to open or close. Equally, all 4 long DREADs write to register 3, which the manual (line 392) confirms is the throwaway pass-through location -- so they are discarded before any coefficient applies. The finding's own CAVEAT and open_questions #1 acknowledge both facts, which is to its credit, but they fatally undercut the 'clearest single error / necessary' framing: a coefficient change to a step that consumes a discarded read and writes to a never-read address is not load-bearing.

Two further problems weaken the proposed decode itself: (1) cs=-127 is mislabeled '-1.0 unity feedback' -- at the confirmed /32 scale it is -3.97, and reading a 7-bit magnitude contradicts the manual's explicit 6-bit multiplier; (2) the proposed bit layout is a non-physical splice of two contradictory field maps (the fieldmap doc vs the owner's net-traced device decode), so 'move XFER/ZERO to lane2' would collide with the confirmed WA/CSIGN bits.

The genuinely useful contribution buried in the finding is the SECOND issue (its caveat): the dead tank is a recirculation/routing problem -- long reads land in R[3]=pass-through and writes/reads never share an address. That aligns with the project memory (Session 10: 'bug = loop gain / feedback routing; line written once, tapped 3x, never re-written'). The coefficient field is at most a secondary decode-plausibility question, not the cause.
verification.corrected_fix:
DROP the coefficient-decode change as the proposed FIX for the dead tank -- it is tested-not-sufficient AND not the cause (writes/reads use disjoint DMEM addresses, so no coefficient can close the loop; long reads land in R[3]=manual-confirmed pass-through). Do not ship the literal 'raw 7-bit lane3 + XFER/ZERO->lane2' change: it reads a 7-bit magnitude that overflows the confirmed 6-bit/32 multiplier, mislabels cs=-127 as -1.0, and splices two contradictory field maps (it would collide with the owner net-traced WA/CSIGN in lane2).

If the coefficient field is to be revisited at all, treat it as a SEPARATE decode-fidelity question, not a tank fix, and resolve it by pin-tracing C0/-C5/ on the T&C schematic (the finding admits this was inferred, not traced). Note the manual evidence is mixed: line 390 says '6-bit multiplier' (favors /32, max mag 63) but line 894 '63/64' is non-integer at /32 -- pin this before changing anything. The current INV_L3=True NOP convention (stored all-FF -> 0) is actually consistent with manual line 364 'all zeros = no-operation' under active-low storage.

REDIRECT effort to the real blocker, which the finding's own caveat and project memory both point at: the recirculation path. Concretely test, in order: (1) the WA/RA bit positions in lane2 -- the long DREADs should land in a NON-pass-through register that a later feedback step reads before it is clobbered; if every long read decodes to WA=3, that bit field is the prime suspect. (2) The offset decode -- a real reverb requires some written address to be re-read; the current disjoint write/read offset sets mean either the offset map is wrong or feedback is via RES/RDRREG (sub-decoder), not DMEM. (3) item #3 (what MEMW actually stores: RES vs the DAB-held RDRREG read content under DAB-RSTB timing). Acceptance unchanged: run_trace(nsamp~89000) must show a monotone-ish decaying energy envelope to -60dB (RT60~2.6s), not 4-5 nonzero samples. Until a variant actually sustains, no decode change should be promoted to the C++ golden.

root_causes: STRUCTURAL LOOP-CLOSURE FAILURE (primary, strongly verified): The recirculating delay tank is never re-fed. Instrumenting run_trace shows EVERY DMEM-write step stores a nonzero value only 1 time in 2000 samples (s40/s44 store 0 even on that one), whereas a sustained delay-line reverb must write a nonzero recirculating value ~2000/2000 samples. Only 5-6 DMEM cells are ever nonzero; total |DM| energy collapses from 132126 (n=1) to 32768 (n=18000) to 0 (n=60000). The impulse writes a few cells once, they are read back ~18000-58000 samples later, but the read result is never routed back into a write, so recirculation stops.

INCOMPLETE FEEDBACK PATH ON THE ALL-PASS CASCADES (primary mechanism behind cause 1): The two nested all-pass cascades (s58-73, s105-124) are the tail generators, but they recirculate ONLY through the 4 registers + the held/floating DAB, which have ZERO cross-sample persistence. Direct test: seeding R[0..3] and ACC with energy and running with no audio/no-DMEM, the energy dies completely in ONE sample (register file is clobbered ~110x/pass; manual 3.7 confirms WA=3 is a scratch pass-through written every clock). The ONLY cross-sample state is DMEM. So the cascades' delayed-sample input must come from a DMEM line and their output must be written back to one, but in the confirmed decode those cascade steps have MI17=0 (NOT DMEM ops) so they never touch DMEM. The result-register feedback (RDRREG, sub=1) that should close the cascade is gated off on almost all of them by the MI4 (offset bit 4) gate on the sub-decoder. Directional proof: forcing the 'idle' cascade steps to source RES (result feedback) raises nonzero output samples 5->29 and extends the tail 46348->77017 and cuts peak 762630->180756 -- the only single change that materially lengthens the tank.

OVER-UNITY / RAILING IS A SECONDARY LARGE-SIGNAL SYMPTOM, NOT THE CAUSE OF DEATH (verified): At impulse=100 (peak energy 4320, nowhere near the +-32768 rail) the tank dies in exactly the same 5 nonzero samples as at impulse=20000. Over-unity therefore cannot be the reason the tank dies; it is a separate artifact of the n=0 transient railing through the 16-step cs~=-32 all-pass cascade when driven hard, plus the esum metric summing |RES| over all 45 XFER steps (internal partial products), which inflates the reported 'peak 762630' ~38x above any single audio sample.

RULED OUT after direct testing (so future effort should NOT go here): (a) MAC pipeline timing -- no_defer, zero-first reorder, and acc-latch variants ALL die at the same ~5 nonzero samples; (b) coefficient decode (inv_l3 / 6-bit-/32 vs raw-7-bit) -- the skeptic's proposed coeff fix was re-run and still dies; (c) the 'write and read offsets are disjoint so no loop can close' claim shared by 3 investigations -- this tested same-SAMPLE address equality, which is the wrong test for a delay line; across TIME reads hit previously-written cells 205108 times over 60000 samples, so the addressing topology is sound; (d) forcing read-offset == write-offset (the timing investigation's proposed fix) makes the tank die FASTER (last_nz->1).

ordered_fixes.change: FIRST verify-then-fix the DAB source on the all-pass-cascade 'idle' steps in tools/aru_datapath.py _dab_source(). The 71 'idle_float' steps return `last` (the held floating bus). The cascade feedback term is supposed to come from the result register (RDRREG, sub==1) but is suppressed by the `st['MI16']==1 and st['MI4']==1` gate (MI4 = offset bit 4, which wrongly couples the DELAY VALUE to device routing). Re-derive the sub-decoder enable from the U47/U48 trace on 060-02475: the prime suspect is that the sub-decoder Y1 enable does NOT require MI4 (or requires a different MI bit). As an experiment, change the gate in _dab_source from `if st['MI16'] == 1 and st['MI4'] == 1:` to `if st['MI16'] == 1:` AND additionally make sub==0 on a cascade step return RES (result feedback) rather than `last`.
ordered_fixes.predicted_effect: Routing result-register feedback into the cascade steps is the only lever that lengthened the tank in testing (nonzero 5->29, last_nz 46348->77017, peak 762630->180756). Predicted: a denser, longer (but not yet 2.6s) tail. This is necessary but likely not sufficient alone; it converts the dead blip into a multi-bounce decay and exposes the next gain/routing issue.
ordered_fixes.support: Verified: the `rdrreg_idle` variant (idle steps source RES) gave peak=180756, nonzero=29, last_nz=77017 vs baseline 762630/5/46348. Dropping only the MI4 gate (without RES on sub==0) did almost nothing (still 5 nonzero), so BOTH the gate and the sub==0->RES routing matter. Manual 3.7 line 390 lists the result register as a valid DAB multiplicand source.

ordered_fixes.change: Make the undriven DAB reset each system cycle instead of holding its last value across steps and samples. In run_trace, the `last` variable persists across the entire run; manual line 939 documents DAB RSTB/ (U20 pin6) as a strobe asserted every system cycle. Test BOTH: (a) reset `last=0` at the top of each sample loop, and (b) reset `last` to RES (the result register) each step. Keep whichever, combined with fix 1, sustains energy.
ordered_fixes.predicted_effect: Removes the spurious cross-sample/cross-step smearing of a single railed DMEM read across the whole register file (the n=18230 event where a railed -32768 from one cell smears everywhere). Cures the large-signal over-injection (peak 762630->~32500 with reset-to-0) WITHOUT being the tail fix by itself. Must be combined with fix 1; on its own reset-to-0 leaves only 1 nonzero sample.
ordered_fixes.support: Verified: reset-undriven-bus-to-0 gives peak 32500 but kills the tail to 1 nonzero sample, confirming it is a correctness/over-unity fix not a tail fix. Manual line 939 documents the per-cycle DAB RSTB/ strobe, contradicting the indefinite-hold model.

ordered_fixes.change: Re-trace the device decode for the all-pass cascade steps against 060-02475 to determine whether they are genuinely register-only (MI17=0) or whether the cascade's nested-all-pass DELAY is actually a short DMEM line. The cascade quads pair steps at IDENTICAL offsets (e.g. s109 & s112 both 0xE984; s58 & s61 both 0xC278) with the textbook all-pass register pattern WA=3,RA=0 / WA=3,RA=1 / WA=1,RA=1 / WA=0,RA=0 -- this is a read-tap-then-write-back delay-line all-pass. If those paired-offset steps should be a DMEM READ (first) and DMEM WRITE (last) of the same cell, the cascade gains its cross-sample memory and the tail closes. Confirm whether MI16/MI17 polarity or the MI4 gate mis-classifies these as idle.
ordered_fixes.predicted_effect: If the paired-offset cascade steps are actually DMEM read/write of a short (~50ms-class) delay line, this supplies the missing cross-sample all-pass memory and is the most likely route to a genuine dense 2.6s tail (matches memory's '50ms comb @ g=0.94 -> 2.6s'). Predicted: sustained decaying envelope once the cascade reads/writes its own DMEM line every sample.
ordered_fixes.support: Verified structurally: s109==s112 offset 0xE984, s58==s61 offset 0xC278, etc., with the canonical 2nd-order all-pass register choreography; register-only recirculation provably cannot persist (seeded energy dies in 1 sample), so the cascade MUST get its delay from DMEM. This reconciles the 'line written once, tapped 3x, never re-written' note in MEMORY.md.

ordered_fixes.change: Only AFTER fixes 1-3 produce a multi-sample decaying envelope: tune the net loop gain. The 16-step cs~=-32(=-1.0) all-pass feedback terms plus the 0x1000-offset writes mean a closed loop will be near-unity; verify the per-loop gain is just under 1.0 so RT60~2.6s (manual ch.4 default Mid 2.0/LF 3.0). Do NOT touch coefficients until the loop demonstrably closes.
ordered_fixes.predicted_effect: Converts a sustained-but-possibly-growing/under-damped tank into a clean monotone-ish decay to -60dB near sample 89000 (RT60 2.6s at 34130 Hz).
ordered_fixes.support: Verified the cascades are dominated by cs=-32 (=-1.0 at /32) all-pass feedback; loop gain is the last free parameter once routing closes. Memory note: 50ms comb @ g=0.94 gives exactly 2.6s, so structure is right and only loop closure/gain remains.

synthesis.try_first: In tools/aru_datapath.py _dab_source(), change the cascade-step DAB source so result-register feedback reaches the all-pass 'idle' steps: replace `if st['MI16'] == 1 and st['MI4'] == 1:` with `if st['MI16'] == 1:` and make the final `return last, False` for gated/idle steps return `RES, False` instead. One-line experiment to confirm: run `python -c "import tools.aru_datapath as A; e=A.run(A.load_microcode(0x01), nsamp=90000, imp=1000); print(max(e), sum(1 for x in e if x), max((i for i,x in enumerate(e) if x), default=-1))"` and check that nonzero-sample count rises well above the baseline 5 (it reaches 29 with RES routed into idle steps) and last_nz extends past 46348 toward 77000+. That confirms result-feedback routing is the loop-closure lever before investing in the full device-decode re-trace.
synthesis.decisive_experiment: To resolve the remaining ambiguity (is the cascade delay register-only-via-held-bus, or a DMEM read/write the decode is missing?), run the cross-sample-persistence test: seed R[0..3]=ACC=RES with energy, run the program with audio_in=0 AND DMEM forced to all-zero, and measure per-sample output energy. RESULT (already run): energy dies completely in ONE sample -> registers/bus have NO cross-sample memory, so the all-pass cascades CANNOT be register-only; their delay MUST be a DMEM line. Therefore the decisive next experiment is: for the cascade quads, re-interpret the two steps that share an identical offset (e.g. s109 & s112 = 0xE984) as a DMEM READ (first occurrence, dab=DM[addr]) and DMEM WRITE-BACK (second occurrence, DM[addr]=RES) of that same cell, then run a 1000-amplitude impulse for 90000 samples. If the per-sample energy envelope becomes a sustained monotone-ish decay (nonzero on most samples, not 5-29 isolated bounces), the missing cross-sample all-pass memory was the DMEM read/write on those paired-offset steps -- which simultaneously explains why register-seeded energy dies, why writes fire only once, and why result-feedback-into-idle only partially helps. If it still produces only sparse bounces, the loop closure is instead purely in the result-register feedback routing (fix 1) and the cascade offsets are genuinely non-DMEM, pointing the trace back to the MI4/sub-decoder gate on 060-02475.
